metadata.title: SSHive — SSH, SFTP, RDP & VNC Client for Mac, iPhone & iPad
metadata.description: Native Apple SSH, SFTP, RDP, VNC and VPN client for Mac, iPhone and iPad. SSH tunnels, broadcast and Touch ID Keychain on every device. Built-in MCP for Claude Code, Cursor and Claude Desktop on macOS.

hero.title: The SSH Client Apple Devices Deserve
hero.subtitle: SSH, SFTP, RDP, VNC, VPN and SSH tunnels — all in one native app on Mac, iPhone and iPad. Free to start. MCP for AI is on macOS.
hero.universalBinary: Native on Mac (Apple Silicon + Intel) · iPhone · iPad

features.title: Everything you need to manage remote servers
features.subtitle: One app. All protocols. Zero switching.

ssh.title: SSH Terminal
ssh.description: GPU-accelerated terminal with xterm.js WebGL. Password, key, and agent authentication. Jump hosts, agent forwarding, and automatic reconnection.

sftp.title: SFTP File Manager
sftp.description: Dual-pane file browser with drag & drop uploads from Finder. Remote file editor, bulk operations, transfer progress with speed indicator.

rdp.title: Remote Desktop (RDP)
rdp.description: Embedded RDP client powered by IronRDP (Rust). Full keyboard, mouse, and clipboard support. No external window — everything stays in SSHive.

vnc.title: VNC Viewer
vnc.description: Built-in VNC client via noVNC. WebSocket proxy integration with full RFB protocol support. Mouse, keyboard, and clipboard.

tunnels.title: SSH Tunnels
tunnels.description: Local forwarding (-L), remote forwarding (-R), and SOCKS5 proxy (-D) with full IPv4/IPv6/domain support. Up to 10 local + 5 remote tunnels per profile.

mcp.title: AI Integration (MCP)
mcp.description: Built-in MCP server lets Claude Code, Cursor, and Claude Desktop access your SSH/SFTP sessions. Copy-paste config, or one-shot CLI for Claude Code.

broadcast.title: Broadcast Mode
broadcast.description: Execute the same command on all connected SSH sessions simultaneously. Perfect for multi-server administration and fleet management.

snippets.title: Quick Commands
snippets.description: Library of reusable commands with 14 presets for system, network, disk, Docker, and Git operations. Create your own custom snippets.

native.title: Native macOS
native.description: Built for macOS with Keychain integration, native notifications, and system theme support. Not a Linux port.

allInOne.title: All-in-One
allInOne.description: SSH, SFTP, RDP, VNC, tunnels, and tools — one app instead of five. Stop switching between windows.

secure.title: Secure & Private
secure.description: macOS Keychain encryption, no telemetry, no ads. Your credentials and data never leave your machine.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Upgrade when you need more.

faq.a5: No. SSHive works completely offline with no account required. Your data stays on your machine, secured by the macOS Keychain.

comparePage.metaTitleTemplate: SSHive vs {competitor} — A {competitor} Alternative for Mac (2026)
comparePage.introTitle: SSHive vs {competitor}: which is the better SSH client for macOS?

detailedIntro.mobaxterm: MobaXterm has been the gold standard all-in-one terminal on Windows for years. With X11 forwarding, embedded SFTP, and RDP support, it set the bar for what a remote management tool should be. SSHive brings this same philosophy to macOS — but with native Keychain security, and modern AI capabilities that MobaXterm does not offer. If you have been searching for a MobaXterm equivalent on Mac, SSHive is built specifically for you. Unlike MobaXterm, which only runs on Windows (or via Wine, with limited success), SSHive is a native macOS application built with Electron and React, optimized for Apple Silicon, and available on the Mac App Store.
detailedIntro.iterm2: iTerm2 has earned its reputation as the best terminal emulator for macOS, and rightfully so. It offers split panes, profiles, advanced search, and incredible customization. However, iTerm2 is purely a terminal emulator — it does not include built-in SFTP file management, RDP, VNC, SSH tunnel management, or connection profiles. SSHive respects what iTerm2 does well while filling the gaps that server administrators and DevOps engineers need. If your workflow involves more than just typing commands in a shell, SSHive consolidates everything into one window.
detailedIntro.terminus: Tabby (formerly Terminus) is a modern, cross-platform terminal built with web technologies. It offers a polished UI, plugin system, and basic SSH features. However, its cross-platform focus means it lacks the deep macOS integration that SSHive provides. SSHive includes full SFTP file management, embedded RDP and VNC clients, SSH tunnel configuration, and AI integration — features that Tabby either lacks entirely or only partially supports through plugins. For macOS users who want a complete remote management experience, SSHive offers significantly more out of the box.
detailedIntro.putty: PuTTY is one of the oldest and most recognized SSH clients in the world. Originally built for Windows, it has been ported to macOS, but the experience is far from native. PuTTY lacks SFTP, RDP, VNC, modern theming, and connection profile management. SSHive takes everything that made PuTTY beloved — simplicity and reliability — and wraps it in a modern, native macOS experience with all the protocols and tools a system administrator needs in 2026.
detailedIntro.termius: Termius is a polished, cross-platform SSH client known for its beautiful UI and sync capabilities. However, its free tier is limited, and advanced features like SSH tunnels require a $10/month subscription. SSHive offers comparable features — SSH, SFTP, connection profiles, Keychain security — with a one-time $9.99 Pro upgrade for unlimited sessions, RDP, VNC, broadcast mode, and AI integration. For macOS users who want professional SSH tooling without recurring costs, SSHive is the clear choice.
detailedIntro.royal-tsx: Royal TSX is a well-established remote management tool for macOS. It supports SSH, RDP, VNC, and SFTP through a plugin architecture. However, Royal TSX costs $29 for a single license and does not include AI integration. SSHive provides all the same protocols in a single, unified app that costs a fraction of the price and includes modern features like MCP-based AI integration for Claude Code and Cursor.
detailedIntro.securecrt: SecureCRT by VanDyke Software has been the enterprise-grade SSH client of choice for decades. It offers advanced scripting, session management, and protocol support. However, at $119 per license, it is priced for enterprise budgets. SSHive provides comparable SSH and SFTP functionality, adds RDP, VNC, broadcast mode, and AI integration, and costs either nothing (free tier) or $9.99 for the Pro upgrade. For individual developers, sysadmins, and small teams, SSHive delivers enterprise-quality features at an accessible price point.

about.title: About SSHive
about.metaDescription: Learn about SSHive, the SSH/SFTP/RDP client for macOS built by Lucas Russo. Inspired by MobaXterm.
about.description: SSHive is built by Lucas Russo, a developer passionate about building great tools for macOS. Inspired by MobaXterm, SSHive brings the same all-in-one experience to Mac users — with a native feel, Keychain security, and modern AI integrations.
about.storyText: As a developer and sysadmin who switched from Windows to macOS, I missed the all-in-one experience that MobaXterm provided. I found myself juggling between Terminal, Cyberduck, Microsoft Remote Desktop, and various other tools. SSHive was born from that frustration — a single app that combines SSH, SFTP, RDP, and VNC with a native macOS feel. Built with Electron, React, and TypeScript, SSHive is crafted with care for security and performance.
techItems: Electron 33 — Native macOS integration with Keychain, notifications, and system theme
React + TypeScript — Type-safe UI with Zustand state management
Tailwind CSS — Responsive design with dark and light themes
ssh2 — Full SSH/SFTP protocol implementation in JavaScript
IronRDP (Rust) — Embedded RDP client via agent-rdp
noVNC — Built-in VNC viewer with WebSocket proxy
Zod — Runtime validation for all IPC communication

docs.title: Documentation
docs.metaDescription: SSHive documentation and frequently asked questions. Learn how to install, configure, and use SSHive on macOS.
docs.subtitle: Frequently asked questions about SSHive.

faq.a1: You can get SSHive from the Mac App Store. SSHive supports macOS 13 Ventura and later.
faq.a2: Yes. The free version includes SSH terminal, SFTP file manager, 2 concurrent sessions, and 5 saved profiles. You can optionally upgrade to Pro ($9.99, one-time) for unlimited sessions, RDP, VNC, tunnels, and more.
faq.q3: Does SSHive work on Intel Macs?
faq.a3: Yes. SSHive on the Mac App Store is a Universal Binary — it runs natively on both Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3/M4) and Intel x64 Macs.
faq.q4: How do I import my SSH config?
faq.a4: Go to the connection dialog, click the import button, and select "Import from ~/.ssh/config". SSHive will parse your SSH config file and create profiles for each host entry.
faq.a5: Yes. Passwords and private key passphrases are encrypted using the macOS Keychain via Electron's safeStorage API. SSHive has no telemetry, no analytics, and no network calls besides your SSH/SFTP/RDP/VNC connections. All data stays on your machine.
faq.q6: How do SSH tunnels work in SSHive?
faq.a6: You can configure Local (-L), Remote (-R), and SOCKS5 (-D) tunnels per connection profile. Tunnels start automatically when the SSH connection is established. You can set up to 10 local and 5 remote tunnels per profile.
faq.q7: Can I use SSHive with Claude or Cursor?
faq.a7: Yes. SSHive includes a built-in MCP (Model Context Protocol) server compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, and Claude Desktop. Setup is copy-paste (or a single CLI command for Claude Code) — AI assistants can then execute commands and browse files on your remote servers.
faq.a8: Profiles are stored in ~/Library/Application Support/SSHive/profiles.json. Credentials are stored separately in credentials.json, encrypted via the macOS Keychain. You can export and import profiles using the built-in export feature.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDescription: SSHive privacy policy. No personal data collected, no analytics in the app, credentials stored locally via macOS Keychain.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.text:
NetMeSafe ("we", "us", "our") operates the SSHive application for macOS (the "App") and the website sshive.app (the "Website"). This Privacy Policy explains how we handle information when you use our App and Website.

By using SSHive, you agree to the practices described in this policy.

dataCollection.title: Data Collection in the App
dataCollection.text:
SSHive does not collect, transmit, or store any personal data on our servers. The App works entirely offline — the only network connections are those you initiate yourself (SSH, SFTP, RDP, VNC).

We do not collect:
— Names, email addresses, or contact information
— Usage analytics or telemetry
— Crash reports
— Device identifiers or advertising IDs
— Location data
— Browsing or search history

localData.title: Data Stored Locally on Your Device
localData.text:
SSHive stores the following data locally on your Mac, in the application support directory (~/Library/Application Support/SSHive/):

— Connection profiles: hostnames, ports, usernames, and connection settings you configure.
— Credentials: passwords and private key passphrases, encrypted via the macOS Keychain using Electron's safeStorage API. These are protected by your macOS login password and never leave your device.
— Application preferences: theme, window size, and other display settings.

This data is never transmitted to our servers or any third party. You can delete it at any time by removing the application and its support files.

website.title: Website Analytics
website.text:
The SSHive website (sshive.app) does not use any analytics, telemetry, or tracking code. There are no cookies, no third-party scripts, and no fingerprinting. We do not know who visits the site, which pages you read, or how long you stay.

The App itself also contains zero analytics, telemetry, or tracking code.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.text:
The SSHive App does not integrate with any third-party analytics, advertising, or tracking services. The App does not send data to any external server other than the remote hosts you explicitly connect to.

The App includes an optional MCP (Model Context Protocol) server feature that, when enabled by you, allows AI assistants (such as Claude or Cursor) running on your local machine to interact with your SSH/SFTP sessions. This communication happens entirely on your local machine and does not transit through our servers.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.text: SSHive is not directed at children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. Since the App collects no personal data from any user, there is no risk of children's data being collected.

security.title: Security
security.text:
We take the security of your data seriously:

— Credentials are encrypted using the macOS Keychain (or iOS Keychain on iPhone/iPad), gated by Touch ID where supported.
— All remote connections (SSH, SFTP) use industry-standard encryption protocols.
— No data is transmitted to our servers, eliminating the risk of server-side breaches.
— The App is code-signed and distributed exclusively via the Apple App Store (Mac App Store on macOS, App Store on iPhone/iPad).

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.text: Since we do not collect any personal data, there is no data to retain on our servers. Data stored locally on your device persists until you delete it by removing your SSHive profiles or uninstalling the application.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.text:
Since SSHive does not collect or store any personal data on our servers, there is no personal data for us to provide, modify, or delete. All your data is stored locally on your device and is under your full control.

You can at any time:
— View your stored profiles in ~/Library/Application Support/SSHive/
— Delete any or all of your stored data
— Uninstall the application to remove all local data

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with an updated revision date. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.text:
If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or our privacy practices, please contact us at:

Email: contact@netmesafe.com
Website: https://sshive.app/contact

featuresPage.title: Features
featuresPage.subtitle: Everything you need to manage remote servers, in one native macOS app.

ssh.metaTitle: SSH Terminal for macOS — GPU Accelerated
ssh.metaDescription: SSHive's SSH terminal features GPU-accelerated rendering, jump host support, and automatic reconnection. Free for macOS.
bullets: GPU-accelerated rendering with xterm.js WebGL for smooth scrolling even with massive output
Password, private key, and SSH agent authentication methods
Jump Host (ProxyJump) support for multi-hop connections through bastion servers
Automatic reconnection when connections drop unexpectedly

sftp.metaTitle: SFTP File Manager for macOS — Drag & Drop
sftp.metaDescription: SSHive's SFTP file manager features drag & drop uploads, a built-in remote editor, and bulk file operations. Free for macOS.
bullets: Dual-pane file browser with breadcrumb navigation and directory bookmarks
Drag & drop upload directly from Finder with real-time transfer progress
Built-in remote file editor — double-click to edit, Cmd+S to save back to server
Bulk operations: select multiple files for download, delete, or chmod

rdp.metaTitle: RDP Remote Desktop for macOS — Embedded Client
rdp.metaDescription: SSHive's embedded RDP client is powered by IronRDP (Rust) with WebSocket JPEG streaming. No external app needed. Free for macOS.
bullets: Embedded client powered by IronRDP (Rust) — no external app needed
WebSocket JPEG streaming for responsive remote desktop experience
Full keyboard and mouse support including Cmd+V paste
Multi-resolution support adapting to your display

vnc.metaTitle: VNC Viewer for macOS — Built-in Client
vnc.metaDescription: SSHive's built-in VNC viewer uses noVNC with full RFB protocol support. No separate relay needed. Free for macOS.
bullets: Built-in VNC viewer via noVNC with full RFB protocol support
WebSocket proxy in the app — no separate VNC relay needed
Mouse, keyboard, and clipboard integration
Works with any VNC server (TightVNC, RealVNC, etc.)

tunnels.metaTitle: SSH Tunnels for macOS — Port Forwarding & SOCKS5
tunnels.metaDescription: SSHive supports local forwarding (-L), remote forwarding (-R), and SOCKS5 dynamic proxy (-D) with full IPv4/IPv6 support. Free for macOS.
bullets: Local forwarding (-L): Access remote services as if they were local
Remote forwarding (-R): Expose local services to the remote network
SOCKS5 dynamic proxy (-D): Full IPv4, IPv6, and domain name routing
Up to 10 local + 5 remote tunnels per connection profile

mcp.metaTitle: MCP AI Integration for macOS — Claude & Cursor
mcp.metaDescription: Claude Code, Cursor and Claude Desktop can hit your live SSH/SFTP sessions through SSHive's built-in MCP server. Copy-paste config or one-shot CLI. Free for macOS.
bullets: Built-in MCP server for AI assistant integration
Compatible with Claude Code (CLI/IDE), Cursor, and Claude Desktop
7 tools: ssh_list_sessions, ssh_execute, sftp_list, sftp_read_file, sftp_write_file, sftp_write_file_chunk, sftp_write_from_local_path
Bearer token authentication with auto-generated tokens

broadcast.metaTitle: Broadcast Mode for macOS — Multi-Server Commands
broadcast.metaDescription: SSHive's broadcast mode sends commands simultaneously to all connected SSH sessions. Perfect for fleet management. Free for macOS.
bullets: Send commands simultaneously to all connected SSH sessions
Toggle with Cmd+Shift+B keyboard shortcut
Toast confirmation showing number of sessions reached
Perfect for fleet management and multi-server administration

snippets.metaTitle: Quick Commands for macOS — Snippet Library
snippets.metaDescription: SSHive includes 14 built-in quick commands for system, network, disk, Docker, and Git, plus custom snippet creation. Free for macOS.
bullets: 14 built-in quick commands for system, network, disk, Docker, and Git
Create, edit, and delete custom snippets
Searchable library with category filtering
One-click insertion into the active terminal

mcpPage.metaTitle: MCP AI Integration — Claude Code, Cursor & Claude Desktop | SSHive
mcpPage.metaDescription: SSHive's built-in MCP server lets AI assistants like Claude Code, Cursor, and Claude Desktop access your SSH and SFTP sessions. Copy-paste config or one-shot CLI command — local and secure.
mcpPage.heroSubtitle: SSHive includes a built-in MCP server that lets Claude Code, Cursor, and Claude Desktop interact with your SSH and SFTP sessions — directly from your machine.

mockup.settingsDesc: Lets AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, Cline…) access active SSH sessions.
mockup.enableServerDesc: Starts a local HTTP server on 127.0.0.1
mockup.claudePrompt: List all running Docker containers on my production server
mockup.claudeThinking: Using SSHive MCP to access session "NMS"…
mockup.claudeResponse: Your production server has 3 containers running: api-gateway, web-frontend, and postgres-db — all up for 14 days with no issues.
mockup.sftpPrompt: Show me the Nginx config for the API
mockup.sftpResponse: Your API reverse proxy is configured on api.example.com (port 443) and forwards traffic to localhost:3000. The SSL configuration looks standard.

whatIsMcp.label: What is MCP?
whatIsMcp.title: Model Context Protocol — the bridge between AI and your infrastructure
whatIsMcp.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is an open standard created by Anthropic that allows AI assistants to interact with external tools and data sources. SSHive implements an MCP server that exposes your active SSH and SFTP sessions as tools — so Claude can execute commands, read files, and browse your remote servers without leaving the conversation.

fast.title: Instant access
fast.desc: No copy-paste, no context switching. Your AI assistant directly queries your servers through SSHive's active sessions.

secure.title: 100% local
secure.desc: The MCP server runs on 127.0.0.1 with Bearer token authentication. Nothing leaves your machine — no cloud relay, no third-party proxy.

auto.title: Copy-paste setup
auto.desc: Enable MCP in Settings, then copy the JSON config into your AI client. Claude Code users can run a single CLI command.

demoSsh.label: SSH via AI
demoSsh.title: Execute commands through natural language
demoSsh.description: Ask Claude to check Docker containers, view logs, restart services, or run any command on your remote servers. SSHive's MCP server bridges the conversation to your live SSH sessions.
demoSsh.bullet1: Claude picks the right session automatically from your active connections
demoSsh.bullet2: Full stdout/stderr output returned directly in the conversation
demoSsh.bullet3: Works with any SSH session — no special server-side setup needed

demoSftp.label: SFTP via AI
demoSftp.title: Browse and read remote files with AI
demoSftp.description: Claude can list directories, read configuration files, and even write files on your remote servers through SSHive's SFTP tools. Perfect for debugging config issues or reviewing logs.
demoSftp.bullet1: List directory contents on any active SFTP session
demoSftp.bullet2: Read remote files — configs, logs, scripts — directly in Claude's context
demoSftp.bullet3: Write files back to the server for quick fixes

tools.label: MCP Tools
tools.title: 7 tools for full server access
tools.subtitle: Each tool maps to a specific SSH or SFTP operation, giving AI assistants precise control over your remote servers.
tools.sshList: Lists all active SSH sessions with host, user, port, and connection status.
tools.sshExecute: Executes a command on a specific SSH session and returns stdout/stderr.
tools.sftpList: Lists files and directories at a given path on a remote server.
tools.sftpRead: Reads the content of a remote file and returns it as text.
tools.sftpWrite: Writes content to a file on the remote server (in-memory).
tools.sftpWriteChunk: Appends base64 chunks (4 MB each) for large files.
tools.sftpWriteFromLocal: Streams a local file directly to the remote — zero base64 overhead, best for large transfers.

setup.label: Setup
setup.title: Ready in 3 clicks
setup.subtitle: Copy the ready-to-paste config into your AI client, or run the one-shot Claude Code CLI command below.

step1.title: Open SSHive Settings
step1.desc: Navigate to Settings > MCP in the SSHive sidebar.

step2.title: Enable the MCP server
step2.desc: Toggle the switch to activate the local MCP server on port 49422. A Bearer token is automatically generated and shown in Settings — you'll paste it into the snippets below.

step3.title: Paste the config — or run the Claude Code one-liner
step3.desc: For Claude Desktop, Cursor and Cline: copy the JSON config and paste it into your client's MCP settings file. For Claude Code (CLI + IDE), copy the one-shot command — it registers SSHive in `~/.claude.json` in a single step. Restart your client and you're connected.

clients.label: Compatible Clients
clients.title: Works with your favorite AI tools
clients.claudeCode: One-shot CLI command — `claude mcp add` registers SSHive in ~/.claude.json in a single line.
clients.cursor: Paste the JSON snippet into Cursor's MCP settings file — SSHive shows the exact config in Settings.
clients.claudeDesktop: Paste the JSON snippet into Claude Desktop's config file (claude_desktop_config.json).
clients.cline: Compatible via manual Streamable HTTP configuration.

security.title: Secure by design
security.description: The MCP server never exposes your sessions to the internet. Everything stays on your machine, protected by authentication and local-only binding.
security.bullet1: Server binds to 127.0.0.1 only — not reachable from the network
security.bullet2: Bearer token authentication on every request
security.bullet4: No cloud relay — direct local communication
security.bullet6: MCP server can be disabled at any time

cta.title: Give your AI superpowers
cta.description: Download SSHive and connect Claude Code, Cursor, or Claude Desktop to your servers in seconds.

aiHero.label: AI-Ready
aiHero.title: Your SSH sessions, accessible to AI
aiHero.subtitle: Enable MCP in Settings, then paste SSHive's config (or run the one-shot Claude Code CLI) — your AI client now calls your live SSH and SFTP sessions through SSHive's built-in MCP server.
aiHero.serversDetail: Active SSH and SFTP sessions in SSHive
aiHero.tools: ssh_list_sessions · ssh_execute · sftp_list · sftp_read_file · sftp_write_file · sftp_write_file_chunk · sftp_write_from_local_path
